reviews.review_msg: Professor Olyaei is an amazing prof and easily one of the best profs I've had. She did an amazing job in making the lectures and course material easy to understand and gave great feedback despite the course being online. She is also very accessible for help outside of class and wants her students to succeed. Lectures and textbook are important.

Professor Olyaei was one of the most caring profs I've had at SFU. She wants her students to excel and helps them out by giving clear instructions, being open to class suggestions, and is very accessible for help. Although the class content was quite dense &detailed, the readings and lectures were very clear! Would definitely recommend this course!

Dr. Olyaei is a very understanding and caring professor who tries her best to ensure all students' needs are met. The assignments and tests are graded fairly easily, and all exam contents are derived from the text book. The text from Webber is easy to understand, straightforward and concise. If you do your readings, you're guaranteed a good grade.

She is a great professor: clear, caring, sweet. She wants everyone's questions to be answered so she encourages talking with her through email or office hours. She is inspirational, articulate, passionate, and approachable. The course structure is quite well organized. It's not hard to do well.

He's obviously intelligent, although his intellectual capacity is not usally demonstrated at a particularly high level in class lectures. He takes a very long time to get to obvious conclusions, though this may be for some pedagogical reason unknown to me. I find him strange, and a little awkward, I prefer to approach him in email vs.in person.

Great guy! Has a laid-back style that is welcomed. Lectures are not the most well-organised but insights are all there and he eventually gets thing across clearly.  Does well at facilitating debate; not afraid to discuss things outside of class content which is great; I like the personal anecdotes...Resembles Christian Bale to a certain extent...

I really do enjoy his class. I find his insights to be interesting, he facilitates interesting discussions, and the readings he assigns are informative and insightful. Being a Poli Sci major, I've had my fill of AWFUL profs, I must say that this man is a nice change of pace!

The course was blended as lecture were online and tutorial was in person. Dr. Heard was very accommodating during the term for my circumstances. His response time was fast. The lectures were informative, engaging and interesting. Assignments and exams were fair. I highly recommend.

He reminds me of Tigger. He is so friendly and makes learning about constitutional law very entertaining and easy to understand, which is impressive given the complexity of Canadian federalism and constitutional interpretation. Graded fairly, recorded lectures, but messed up both our midterm and final, so that was slightly frustrating.
